overrides.override_summary: All initial fees deferred until pre-opening obligations completed and franchisee open for business
overrides.why_it_matters: Franchisee does not bear full financial risk until franchisor performs; protects against inadequate capitalization

overrides.override_summary: Post-term noncompete may not be enforceable under CA law
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisees may be able to operate similar business after termination without restriction

overrides.override_summary: Louisiana governing law provision may not be enforceable under CA law
overrides.why_it_matters: CA courts may apply CA law instead of LA law to franchise disputes

overrides.override_summary: Maximum interest rate 10% annually
overrides.why_it_matters: 12% compounding rate in FA may be capped at 10% for CA franchisees

overrides.override_summary: No waiver of claims under state franchise law, including fraud in the inducement
overrides.why_it_matters: Integration/merger clause cannot bar franchisee from asserting state law claims

overrides.override_summary: Bankruptcy termination provision may not be enforceable under federal bankruptcy law
overrides.why_it_matters: Bankruptcy filing alone may not trigger termination for CA franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Illinois law governs the franchise agreement
overrides.why_it_matters: IL law supersedes LA law for IL franchisees — different legal standards apply

overrides.override_summary: Venue designation outside Illinois is void per IL Franchise Disclosure Act Section 4
overrides.why_it_matters: IL franchisee can litigate in IL rather than traveling to LA

overrides.override_summary: Separately flags franchisor financial condition concern per Item 21
overrides.why_it_matters: IL regulator independently determined financial condition is a material risk

overrides.override_summary: Any condition purporting to waive compliance with IL franchise law is void
overrides.why_it_matters: Broad anti-waiver protection for IL franchisees

overrides.override_summary: General release on renewal/transfer does not apply to claims under MD Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law
overrides.why_it_matters: MD franchisees preserve MD franchise law claims even when signing release at renewal/transfer

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may sue in Maryland for MD franchise law claims
overrides.why_it_matters: MD franchisees can bring claims locally rather than in LA

overrides.override_summary: Claims under MD law must be brought within 3 years of franchise grant
overrides.why_it_matters: 3-year statute of limitations overrides 1-year contractual limit

overrides.override_summary: Release, estoppel, or waiver provisions do not apply to MD franchise law liability
overrides.why_it_matters: MD franchise law protections cannot be waived in franchise documents

overrides.override_summary: 90 days termination notice with 60 days to cure; 180 days non-renewal notice
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchisees get substantially longer cure period than 14-day contractual provision

overrides.override_summary: Franchisor must protect franchisee's right to use marks and indemnify against infringement claims
overrides.why_it_matters: MN creates affirmative franchisor obligation that does not exist in base FA

overrides.override_summary: No release relieves franchisor from MN franchise law liability
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchisees preserve all MN franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: Liquidated damages provision (Section 37 H.3) deleted entirely
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchisees not liable for remaining-term Monthly Fees on early termination

overrides.override_summary: Rights under NY GBL Article 33 preserved through releases (GBL 687.4, 687.5 non-waiver)
overrides.why_it_matters: NY franchise law claims survive release at renewal/transfer

overrides.override_summary: Injunction provisions modified to 'may seek' rather than automatic consent
overrides.why_it_matters: NY franchisees retain right to contest injunction applications

overrides.override_summary: ND law supersedes LA law where conflicting
overrides.why_it_matters: ND law governs franchise disputes for ND franchisees

overrides.override_summary: All venue designations outside ND deleted
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchisees cannot be forced to litigate in LA

overrides.override_summary: General release not required on renewal
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchisees renew without waiving past claims

overrides.override_summary: Noncompetes generally considered unenforceable per ND Century Code Section 9-08-06
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchisees may operate similar business after termination

overrides.override_summary: Liquidated damages, jury waiver, and punitive damages waiver all deleted; statute of limitations per ND law
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchisees retain full damages rights, jury trial, and statutory limitation periods

overrides.override_summary: Good cause required for termination with 30-day cure period; fair compensation on non-renewal
overrides.why_it_matters: MI law provides substantial termination/non-renewal protections beyond base FA

overrides.override_summary: Provision requiring arbitration/litigation outside MI is void
overrides.why_it_matters: MI franchisees cannot be forced out of state for dispute resolution

overrides.override_summary: Cannot refuse transfer except for good cause
overrides.why_it_matters: Franchisor consent to transfer must be based on reasonable criteria
